title:
Lesson 1:
I am, You are
description: The verb 'ser' (to be) is fundamental. 'Soy' = I am, 'Eres' = you are (informal). Used for identity and characteristics.

title:
Lesson 2:
What is your name?
description: '¿Cómo te llamas?' literally means 'How do you call yourself?' It's the standard way to ask someone's name. 'Me llamo' = My name is (I call myself).

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: My name is María. And you?

title:
Lesson 3:
Where are you from?
description: '¿De dónde eres?' asks where you're from. 'Soy de...' = I am from... This builds on 'ser' from Lesson 1.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Hello Pedro. Where are you from?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I am from Mexico. And you?

title:
Lesson 4:
How are you?
description: '¿Cómo estás?' uses 'estar' (to be) for temporary states/feelings. 'Estoy' = I am. Different from 'ser' which is for permanent traits.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I am well, thank you. And you?

title:
Lesson 5:
This is...
description: 'Este es' (masculine) or 'Esta es' (feminine) means 'This is'. Used to introduce people or things. Gender agreement is important in Spanish.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Hello Luis, nice to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Y esta es mi amiga, Rosa.
dialogue.english: And this is my friend, Rosa.
